items.title: Business sentiment slides as geopolitical risks bite: RAM survey
items.summary: Its Business Confidence Index fell to 33.4 in the second quarter from 42.2 in the first quarter, marking the second straight quarter of pessimistic business sentiment. Concerns over weak economic conditions remained high, although they eased slightly to 66 per cent from 71 per cent previously. Logistics and shipping were the hardest-hit cost components, cited by 69 per cent of respondents, followed by raw materials (64 per cent) and fuel and energy costs (50 per cent).

items.title: Capital A's ADE gains recognition at MAIA's President Award
items.summary: Meanwhile, ADE chief executive officer Mahesh Kumar was presented with the Aerospace Premiership Award, which honours individuals who have demonstrated leadership, innovation and contributions to the aerospace sector. The awards, introduced by the Malaysia Aerospace Industry Association (MAIA), recognise aerospace companies and individuals that have showed excellence in management practices, product and service quality and stakeholder value creation. Mahesh said the recognition reflects ADE's commitment to building a culture of excellence, innovation and continuous learning while developing world-class aerospace talent.

items.title: Police bust syndicate using Malaysia as processing hub for MDMA vape cartridges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have busted a foreigner-run drug syndicate allegedly using Malaysia as a processing and packaging hub to smuggle Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA)-laced vape cartridges to Indonesia via maritime routes. "We also seized 500 plastic packets containing vape cartridges suspected to contain MDMA, weighing nearly 5kg, as well as eight small plastic bottles believed to contain liquid MDMA," he said, adding that the total value of the drugs seized was estimated at RM152,400. Hussein said the syndicate deliberately operated from remote locations, including village houses, to process the drugs before transporting them by sea to Indonesia.

items.title: Anwar touched by boy’s plantation journey to meet him in Segamat, gifts bicycle after visit
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared the heartwarming story of nine-year-old Muhammad Afif Ikhwan, who travelled through an oil palm plantation with his grandmother in the hope of meeting him. “What may appear to us as a small dream was, in fact, big enough to move his grandmother, Rosimah Mohammad, to ride a motorcycle through an oil palm plantation to fulfil her grandson’s wish. Yesterday, Bernama reported that Rosimah, 58, from Kampung Pudu, Segamat, rode a motorcycle along oil palm plantation tracks solely to give her grandson the opportunity to see the Prime Minister up close.

items.title: Death calculator: DOSM's new tool reveals top health risks by demographic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Statistics Department's (DOSM) latest interactive calculator is giving Malaysians an insight into the leading causes of death among people of similar age and gender. Rather than predicting an individual's fate, the calculator highlights the health risks commonly faced by people within the same demographic group. For example, ischaemic heart disease may emerge as the leading cause of death among men approaching their 40s, while pneumonia may rank highest among younger women.

items.title: PM: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao road link to spur Malaysia-Thailand border economic growth
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 10 — The new road alignment linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah and the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand will serve as a catalyst to propel economic growth in both regions, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the project represented a historic milestone in Malaysia-Thailand relations and would strengthen cross-border connectivity, trade activities and economic cooperation between the two countries. Anwar said the initiative was part of broader efforts to establish a special economic border zone involving northern Malaysia and southern Thailand to spur economic growth and improve the livelihoods of communities in the border areas.

items.title: Penang police to intensify crackdown as World Cup enters final stages
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang police will intensify operations against illegal betting as the FIFA World Cup enters its final stages, with 31 people arrested statewide so far. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the arrests were made during raids on 26 premises, adding that six of those detained were Chinese nationals. Illegal betting can take place anywhere.

items.title: Chegubard disqualified from Negri Sembilan polls after RM5,000 sedition fine
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Political activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, has been disqualified from contesting in the Negri Sembilan state election after the Sessions Court here today fined him RM5,000 for publishing seditious content. His lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, said the fine rendered Chegubard ineligible to stand as an election candidate under Article 48 of the Federal Constitution. He said the defence respected the court's decision.

items.title: Private college employee charged with sexual assault, sodomy of two teens
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A private college employee pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to three counts of committing physical sexual assault and carnal intercourse against nature against two teenage boys. Fikri Zainal Abidin, 38, claimed trial after the charges were read before judge Awang Kerisnada Awang Mahmud. According to the charges, the accused committed physical sexual assault against two 15-year-old boys and intentionally committed carnal intercourse against the order of nature against one of them.

items.title: Farmers' group warns against making growers easy targets under new rice bills
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tougher laws governing the padi and rice sector must not make farmers easy enforcement targets while powerful industry players escape scrutiny, a farmers' group warned. Malaysian Padi Farmers Brotherhood Organisation (Pesawah) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said stronger action was needed to curb smuggling, supply hoarding, grade falsification, and market manipulation, which had long harmed farmers and consumers. However, he said enforcement must be fair and cover the entire supply chain, including millers, wholesalers, importers, and retailers.

items.title: Netanyahu pushes controversial bills in final pre-election dash
items.summary: JERUSALEM: With the Israeli parliament set to dissolve in the coming days, just four months before national elections, the government of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is racing to pass a series of bills, drawing fierce criticism from opposition. Seven pieces of legislation are currently on the agenda of the Knesset, Israel's 120-seat parliament, each a priority for one or more parties in Netanyahu's ruling coalition. He argued that only his newly formed party, Yashar (Straight) party, would be capable of forming the next government.

items.title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao road to boost border economy, says Anwar
items.summary: hifzuddin@bh.com.my Pix by Wan Nabil Nasir BUKIT KAYU HITAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is confident that the opening of the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam border complex with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex will boost the economic potential between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly in the border regions. He said the new route is expected to resolve the traffic congestion previously experienced at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complex and the Sadao CIQ complex. "We have gone beyond normal diplomatic relations by demonstrating our commitment to establishing a Special Border Economic Zone covering northern Malaysia and southern Thailand to spur economic growth," he said.

items.title: Chegubard disqualified from Negeri Sembilan polls after RM5,000 fine in sedition case
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 10 — Activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, can no longer contest in the upcoming Negeri Sembilan election following a RM5,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court today, after he was found guilty of publishing seditious material two years ago. His lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, said the defence respected the court’s decision but would be filing an appeal soon, Berita Harian (BH) reported. “However, with this RM5,000 fine, Chegubard is disqualified from contesting.

items.title: Indonesia says landfill fire near Jakarta extinguished
items.summary: JAKARTA: A landfill fire on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital, Jakarta, has been put out after burning for more than a week as evacuees returned home, a disaster agency spokesman said on Friday. The Jatiwaringin landfill in the Tangerang district west of Jakarta caught fire on June 30, prompting the local government to declare a two-week emergency. The fire – which burned an area at the landfill measuring around 15 hectares (37 acres) – had been completely extinguished by Thursday evening, National Disaster Mitigation Agency spokesman Abdul Muhari said in a statement.

items.title: Asean to discuss engagement with Myanmar at special meeting, Philippines says
items.summary: MANILA: An upcoming meeting between foreign ministers of the Southeast Asian grouping Asean and their counterpart from Myanmar will tackle the country's civil war and its engagement with the bloc after five years in the cold, the Philippines said on Friday. Myanmar's military denies those allegations. The Philippine foreign ministry stressed Myanmar remained an integral part of Asean and Sunday's meeting would be informal, giving Myanmar's foreign minister a chance to brief counterparts on the situation in the country.

items.title: Loke orders action against all helmetless campaigners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has been ordered to issue summonses to anyone found riding motorcycles without helmets while campaigning in the Johor election. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the department must not practise double standards when enforcing the law, including against members of parties in the government. Loke said the principle applied to everyone without exception, including PH candidates and campaign workers.

items.title: Anwar, Anutin launch new road linking Bukit Kayu Hitam and Sadao border complexes
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his Thai counterpart, Anutin Charnvirakul, today launched the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam border complex with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex. The Home Ministry said the launch reflects the strong commitment of both countries to strengthening diplomatic ties, enhancing cross-border strategic cooperation, and improving regional connectivity. "The opening of the new road marks a strategic milestone that reflects the success of close cooperation between Malaysia and Thailand in developing a more modern, integrated and resilient border infrastructure," the ministry said.

items.title: Police smash synthetic drug syndicate supplying Klang Valley, Kuantan clubs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a synthetic drug syndicate supplying entertainment outlets in the Klang Valley and Kuantan, seizing RM13.2 million worth of drugs in a series of raids. He said the operation involved five raids around the Klang Valley and Pahang, targeting condominium residential units that were used as locations to store and package drugs. "In the same raids, police also seized various types of drugs, including 84.3kg of Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) powder, 4.4kg of ecstasy pills, 2.4kg of ketamine and 730g of Erimin 5 pills.

items.title: Landslide kills five in Philippines as biggest typhoon in decades nears Taiwan
items.summary: KEELUNG: Five people were killed in a landslide in the Philippines, and hundreds in Taiwan were evacuated from their homes, as the biggest typhoon in decades swept towards the region on Friday. Typhoon Bavi is expected to pound Taiwan's north and east, as well as Japan's remote southwestern islands, on Friday and Saturday before smashing into China, which has been hit by deadly storms this week. Keelung grocery store owner Chang Shih-huo, 76, told AFP.

items.title: UAE's E& to sell Vodafone stake to French telecoms tycoon Niel for nearly US$6bil
items.summary: DUBAI: UAE telecoms group E& said on Friday it would sell its stake in Vodafone for US$5.95 billion to the family group of French billionaire Xavier Niel, ending its investment in the British firm after a review of its international portfolio. The deal prices Vodafone shares at 112.5 pence each, a 15 per cent premium to the last close of 97.76 pence, and will result in a net cash return of about US$1.3 billion, E& said. E& said the shares would be sold simultaneously through off-market block trades to three financial institutions, which would hold them until the Niel family group's acquisition vehicle completed regulatory requirements.

items.title: Kelantan police, MCBA strengthen cooperation at border complexes
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan police will continue working closely with the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) following the agency's takeover of operations at two more Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes in the state. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said MCBA assumed responsibility for the Pengkalan Kubor and Bukit Bunga ICQS complexes earlier this month. "MCBA oversees the ICQS complexes and official border crossings, while police continue enforcement operations outside these areas, including illegal jetties along the Malaysia-Thailand border.

items.title: Kulasegaran launches Perak Bar Mediation Centre, says disputes can be settled in a day
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — Long-running and backlogged civil cases pending before the courts can now be referred to mediation service without being withdrawn, allowing disputes to be resolved in as little as one day. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said mediation services provide faster and more cost-effective way to resolve disputes. Kulasegaran suggested parties involved in pending civil suits do not have to withdraw their cases to pursue mediation, but instead apply to the court for the proceedings to be stayed temporarily and seek the judge’s permission to refer the dispute to mediation.

items.title: Mais: Friday prayers at IOI City Mall allowed temporarily since Sept 2024
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Temporary permission for Friday prayers at Musolla IOI City Mall in Putrajaya has been in effect since Sept 6, 2024, following consent from the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) chairman Senator Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said the approval was granted by Mais through the Selangor State Mosque and Surau Governance Committee. Salehuddin reiterated that the permission granted for Friday prayers at Musolla IOI City Mall is temporary.

items.title: GFM Services bags RM148.2mil PIC turnaround work contracts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: GFM Services Bhd's unit HIMS Integrated Services Sdn Bhd has secured RM148.2 million worth of two release orders for major turnaround works at the Pengerang Integrated Complex (PIC) in Johor. In a statement, the integrated facilities management services provider said the orders were awarded by Pengerang Petrochemical Company Sdn Bhd and Pengerang Refining Company Sdn Bhd under the existing Integrated Turnaround Main Mechanical and Maintenance Mechanical Static (TA4MS) contract. The company's energy division comprises Highbase Strategic Sdn Bhd, HIMS Integrated Services Sdn Bhd — a 51 per cent-owned joint venture with Singapore-based oil and gas engineering company Mun Siong Engineering Limited — and Shapadu CR Asia Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Lianson Fleet buys Ultramax-class vessels for over RM213mil to expand dry bulk shipping ops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lianson Fleet Group Bhd plans to buy two Ultramax-class bulk carriers for US$52.32 million (RM213.2 million) as the company expands beyond its offshore support vessel business and strengthens its dry bulk shipping operations. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Lianson Fleet said its Singapore-based unit Lianson Fleet Pte Ltd (LFPL) had signed two separate memorandum of agreements to buy MV Tian Mu Shan and MV Yan Dang Shan from unrelated companies incorporated in China. Lianson Fleet said the vessels would be its first Ultramax-class bulk carriers, marking a step up from its existing Supramax bulk carrier fleet.

items.title: Seberang Perai council upgrades 70 playgrounds, plans to revitalise 630 more neighbourhood parks
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 10 — The Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) has upgraded about 70 neighbourhood playgrounds in housing estates over the past three years, with plans to continue upgrading the remaining 630 open spaces. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said MBSP currently manages about 700 open spaces in existing housing estates across Seberang Perai. He said these open spaces were handed over by developers of the housing estates many years ago without playgrounds or recreational amenities.

items.title: Perkeso's officers not involved in alleged claims fraud, says CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has reassured the public that its officers were not involved in the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive fraud claims investigated by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). Perkeso group chief executive Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the alleged fraud involved falsified documents submitted by claimants, which initially appeared genuine before being detected through its artificial intelligence (AI)-powered anti-fraud system. "The officers who processed the claims were not involved in the fraud.

items.title: Malaysia records over 42,000 dengue cases, 34 deaths this year amid sharp rise in Klang Valley, says MOH
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — The number of dengue cases recorded nationwide this year rose 34.8 per cent to 42,848 as of yesterday, compared with 31,790 cases recorded during the same period last year. Epidemiological analysis showed that nearly two-thirds, or 63.5 per cent, of all dengue cases in Malaysia were reported in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. Selangor recorded 19,313 cases, a 43.2 per cent increase from the same period last year, while Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya reported 7,909 cases, up 83.8 per cent.

items.title: Banting teen accused of stabbing classmate faces attempted murder charge, court orders psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: BANTING, July 10 — The Magistrates’ Court here today ordered a Form Three student involved in a stabbing incident at a school in Banting, Kuala Langat, on Monday to be sent to Hospital Bahagia for psychiatric assessment. No plea was recorded from the 15 year old accused when the charge was read out by a court interpreter. Earlier, the teenager arrived at court at 8.33am in a police vehicle.

items.title: Bickering during election campaigns reflects mature politics, says Ismail Sabri
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Criticisms traded between parties forming the federal government during the Johor election campaign are normal and reflect political maturity, says Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob. The former prime minister said differences in stance during a campaign should not be viewed as unusual because the parties involved continue to discharge their duties professionally. He said this situation was previously experienced when he led the government, which comprised Umno, Pas, Bersatu, and several other parties.

items.title: Perkeso's Lindung 24 Jam opt-out available from Monday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The online opt-out facility for the Non-Employment Injury Scheme (Lindung 24 Jam) will be available from Monday, said the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso). Its group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said contributors who choose to withdraw from the scheme will be required to complete and sign a liability waiver declaration. "Those who wish to opt out of the scheme must complete what we regard as a liability waiver declaration.

items.title: MOE outlines data‑driven safety assessment covering attendance, behaviour and CCTV in schools, teacher training institutes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) uses data-driven assessments, monitoring, auditing and compliance with established regulations and procedures to evaluate the actual safety level in schools and teacher education institutes (IPG). The MOE said the School Inspectorate assesses the effectiveness of safety measures in schools based on seven key indicators, including three-year student attendance trends, Sistem Sahsiah Diri Murid data, findings from Student Voice and Teacher Voice questionnaires, and surprise inspections. The MOE said the level of school safety is also assessed annually through the School Safety Rating System (SPKS), which involves a self-assessment covering six main aspects, namely Student Activity Management, Infrastructure Safety Management, Social Management, Crisis and Disaster Management, Risk Management, and Security Guard Service Management.

items.title: Tourism Malaysia launches first VM2026 outreach in Timor‑Leste, eyes stronger ties
items.summary: JAKARTA, July 10 — Tourism Malaysia has launched its first tourism promotion initiative in Timor-Leste as part of its drive to attract more visitors under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign while deepening tourism cooperation. As part of MYEXPO Dili 2026 on July 8 and 9, Tourism Malaysia organised the Visit Malaysia 2026 Destination Seminar and Table Top, bringing together travel agents, tour operators and tourism stakeholders from Timor-Leste and Malaysia to develop travel packages and strengthen business partnerships. Tourism Malaysia Jakarta Director Hairi Mohd Yakzan said the initiative reflected the agency’s commitment to expanding its presence in emerging tourism markets while promoting the VM2026 campaign, themed “Surreal Experiences”.

items.title: Flights grounded: Malaysia Airlines halts Kuala Lumpur–Taipei routes July 11 citing Typhoon Bavi
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 10 — Malaysia Airlines has grounded flights between Kuala Lumpur and Taipei tomorrow as Typhoon Bavi barrels towards Taiwan, scrapping two services and urging passengers to rebook or seek refunds. The airline said services MH366 (Kuala Lumpur–Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei–Kuala Lumpur) scheduled for July 11 have been called off due to adverse weather conditions. “Passenger and crew safety remains our utmost priority,” Malaysia Airlines said in the statement today.

items.title: Election Commission targets 70 per cent voter turnout for Johor polls
items.summary: PONTIAN: The Election Commission (EC) expects a 70 per cent voter turnout, translating to 1.8 million voters, for the 16th Johor election tomorrow. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the anticipated increase in turnout compared to the 2022 state election was driven by growing public interest in the state's democratic process. Johor has more than 2.7 million registered voters, with 1,076 polling centres involving 4,849 streams.

items.title: Johor polls: EC expects 70pc turnout from state’s 2.7 million voters, citing campaign‑period enthusiasm
items.summary: PONTIAN, July 10 — The Election Commission (EC) expects voter turnout for tomorrow’s Johor state election to reach around 70 per cent, driven by strong public interest in participating in the democratic process. Ramlan said about 2.7 million registered voters are eligible to cast their ballots in this Johor state election. “There are 17 centres that will close at 2 pm, 42 centres at 4 pm, and 1,114 centres at 6 pm tomorrow,” he said.

items.title: Hadi: PAS made up with BN to preserve Muslim political power in Johor
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 10 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has stressed that the Johor state election will decide the future course of the state’s leadership, urging voters to rally behind Malay-Muslim stewardship while promising justice and prosperity for all communities. He said Malay-Muslim stewardship must be strengthened to preserve Islam’s position while ensuring fairness and wellbeing for citizens of every race and religion. Abdul Hadi explained that cooperation between PAS and Barisan Nasional (BN) in Johor was forged to prevent fragmentation of Muslim political power.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia sharply higher at midday, tracking regional gains
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank gained eight sen to RM10.94, Public Bank rose six sen to RM4.91, Tenaga Nasional added two sen to RM14.32, CIMB was five sen higher at RM7.65, and IHH Healthcare advanced 17 sen to RM8.49. As for the active stocks, Zetrix AI and HHRG were up one sen each to 73.5 sen and 10.5 sen, respectively, Nationgate increased seven sen to 87.5 sen, Tanco inched up half a sen to 19.5 sen, and SRKK AI climbed 1.5 sen to 62.5 sen. Of the top gainers, Fraser & Neave added 76 sen to RM29.68, UMS Integration was 46 sen higher at RM8.22, Petronas Gas edged up 28 sen to RM17.76, Kuala Lumpur Kepong advanced 26 sen to RM21.48, and Nestle rose 22 sen to RM91.52. Malaysian Pacific Industries led the losers list, falling 48 sen to RM45.78, while Vitrox shed 15 sen to RM7.39, SAM Engineering & Equipment slipped 13 sen to RM4.77, Allianz Malaysia lost 10 sen to RM21.02, and Apollo Food declined nine sen to RM5.88.

items.title: SD Property, ESR expand Bukit Raja logistics hub
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd and its joint venture partner ESR are expanding the E-Metro Logistics Park in Bandar Bukit Raja with two new Grade A logistics facilities. This adds more than 2.2 million square feet of warehouse space amid rising demand for modern logistics infrastructure. The completion of Metrohub 4 and the groundbreaking of Metrohub 3 will lift the park's total logistics portfolio to more than 4.2 million sq ft, reinforcing its position as one of Malaysia's key industrial and logistics hubs.

items.title: Selangor Customs busts RM513,000 drug smuggling bids via courier parcels
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor Customs Department foiled three separate attempts to smuggle various types of drugs worth RM513,045 through courier services in April and June. State Customs director Mohamad Azhar Ahmad Paharazi said the first case involved the seizure of 781g of cannabis buds worth RM62,480 during an inspection of an imported courier box at a mail centre in Sepang on April 14. He said the second case on April 24 involved the seizure of 8,965g of syabu and heroin worth RM448,250 during an inspection of two export wooden pallet boxes under a single air waybill at a courier mail centre in Sepang.

items.title: Govt outlines skills, ethics and tech adaptation as three‑pronged approach to help media navigate AI risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The government is focusing on a three-pronged strategy, namely skills development, stronger ethical standards and technological adaptation, to help media practitioners address the challenges and opportunities presented by artificial intelligence (AI). In a written parliamentary reply published on Parliament’s website, the Ministry of Communications said AI represents a major advancement for the media industry, but the government also acknowledged growing concerns over manipulated content, misinformation and deepfake technology, which could undermine public trust in news sources and media institutions. The ministry stressed that the government is not opposed to AI adoption.

items.title: AI emerges as manufacturers' answer to rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As manufacturers contend with rising production costs, supply chain disruptions, labour shortages, geopolitical uncertainties and increasingly demanding customer expectations, many are turning to intelligent, AI-powered solutions to improve resilience, productivity and operational efficiency. The company on Thursday introduced HashMicro X, an upgraded manufacturing solution designed to help businesses automate complex production processes, accelerate operational decision-making and optimise factory performance through artificial intelligence. "Manufacturers generate enormous amounts of operational data every day, but data alone doesn't improve productivity," said Nur Fadiyah Syifa, HashMicro's vice president of strategic communications.

items.title: Anthony Loke directs JPJ to go after campaigners riding motorcycles without helmets in Johor
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has directed the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to issue summonses to individuals seen riding motorcycles without helmets during the Johor state election (PRN Johor) campaign. The order follows viral video showing a group of campaign workers, including those from Loke’s DAP, violating road safety regulations during their convoys. The minister said that these principles apply to all parties without exception, including candidates and campaign workers from his own coalition.

items.title: Bukit Aman bust drug distribution syndicates, seize RM13.3m in raids across three states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Federal narcotics police have dismantled two drug distribution syndicates worth RM13.3 million operating out of Kuala Lumpur and Selangor in separate major operations conducted earlier this month. Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Commissioner Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the operations were carried out at several locations across Selangor, the Klang Valley and Pahang between July 1 and July 3. “Both of these operations represent a significant success for the department in dismantling synthetic drug distribution syndicate networks and foiling attempts to smuggle drugs overseas.

items.title: Husband, wife face multiple charges over alleged fire attacks
items.summary: IPOH: A husband and wife were hit with six criminal charges in three separate courts here today, including three counts of committing mischief by fire. M. Dhinakaran and B. Rahtheneswary, both 34, pleaded not guilty to three counts of committing mischief by fire in separate incidents last month before Sessions Court judge Ainul Shahrin Mohamad. Both charges were framed under Section 436 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which provides for up to 20 years' imprisonment and a fine upon conviction.

items.title: Police bust drop‑and‑collect drug syndicate in Kuching, arrest seven and seize RM1.35m meth
items.summary: KUCHING, July 10 — Police have busted a drug trafficking syndicate believed to have used the drop-and-collect method to distribute drugs, arresting seven individuals and seizing 26.97 kilogrammes (kg) of suspected methamphetamine, or syabu, worth RM1.35 million in four raids around Kuching and Padawan. He said the first raid at a residential area in Jalan Matang resulted in the arrest of a 31-year-old local man and the seizure of 60.5 grammes (g) of suspected methamphetamine. “A search of the apartment uncovered 5.45g of suspected methamphetamine,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Rompin Hospital operations unaffected after non‑concession workers’ protest, salary issue resolved, says Pahang Health Dept
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 10 — Operations at Rompin Hospital and the delivery of healthcare services to patients have not been affected following a gathering by workers of a non-concession company at the hospital, which went viral on social media yesterday, according to the Pahang State Health Department (JKNP). JKNP said in a statement today that the department had taken the necessary measures to ensure the continuity of healthcare services at the hospital. The department said 24 workers from the non-concession company, comprising cleaning and landscape maintenance personnel, were involved in the gathering.

items.title: Selangor reviewing terminal operators as Carey Island port awaits MOT licence for September start, says MB
items.summary: BANDUNG, July 10 — Selangor is ready to break ground on the proposed third port terminal on Carey Island as early as September, subject to the Ministry of Transport’s (MOT) approval of the port licence, as the state reviews potential operators for the terminal, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said. In Malaysia, a port licence refers to the authorisation to carry out maritime, shipping or commercial activities within a port area regulated by statutory authorities such as the Port Klang Authority. MMC Port Holdings Sdn Bhd, the port operations arm of MMC Corp, is the owner of Northport (Malaysia) Bhd, and it is also the operator of Port of Tanjung Pelepas, Johor Port and Tanjung Bruas Port.

items.title: DOSM: Malaysia’s job market stable amid global supply crisis
items.summary: The total labour force grew by 0.1 per cent month-on-month to 17.34 million persons, while the labour force participation rate remained unchanged at 70.9 per cent. Employment saw a marginal increase of 0.1 per cent, reaching 16.82 million persons. Own-account workers also saw a small uptick of 0.1 per cent, totalling 3.15 million.

items.title: MAIS clarifies Friday‑prayer approval at Musala IOI City Mall was granted with Selangor Sultan’s consent in 2024
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 10 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Council (MAIS) has clarified that permission to conduct Friday prayers at the Musala IOI City Mall, Putrajaya, was granted from September 6, 2024, following a decision by the Selangor State Mosque and Surau Governance Committee (JATUMS) and with the consent of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. MAIS chairman Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said the approval was granted after an assessment found that the premises housed a large number of male Muslim workers and received a high number of Muslim visitors, making it difficult for them to perform Friday prayers at nearby mosques. “Furthermore, both mosques are unable to accommodate the large number of congregants attending Friday prayers,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MACC remands 33, including 10 enforcement officers, over visa bribery syndicate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 33 individuals, including enforcement officers and civil servants, in a coordinated operation across Putrajaya and three states, over their suspected involvement in a syndicate linked to various offences involving foreign nationals. According to sources, 10 enforcement officers were arrested for allegedly accepting bribes in exchange for not taking action against foreign workers who had violated the Immigration Act 1959, as well as for issuing visas, passes and permits without following proper procedures. The source said other offences detected included submitting documents containing false particulars for applications and quotas for foreign workers.

items.title: Malaysia’s growth expected to hold at 4-5pc despite external uncertainties, says BNM governor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Malaysia’s economy continues to show resilient growth despite the ongoing West Asia conflict, Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said. In an interview with Bernama, he said high-frequency indicators point to some moderation in economic activity in the second quarter (2Q) of 2026. “We can expect growth to moderate, but it should still be good growth considering global developments,” he said, adding that the central bank remains confident that full-year growth will stay firmly within its 4-5 per cent forecast.

items.title: PM Anwar extends warm 101st birthday wishes to Dr Mahathir
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today extended birthday wishes to former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad, who turns 101, in a Facebook post accompanied by two photographs — one recent and one from their earlier years in government. “Happy 101st birthday to Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad. The post featured a recent image of Dr Mahathir smiling while seated in a wheelchair, accompanied by his wife Tun Dr Siti Hasmah Mohd Ali and several aides.

items.title: BNM governor says OPR adjustments will be data‑dependent on growth and inflation trends
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — At its current level of 2.75 per cent, the overnight policy rate (OPR) remains appropriate and consistent with Malaysia’s growth and inflation trajectory, said Bank Negara Malaysia Governor Datuk Sri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour. He said the central bank will continue to calibrate its monetary policy based on evolving growth and inflation trends, with any future adjustment to the benchmark rate to depend on incoming economic data. He said BNM monitors economic conditions continuously using Statistics Department Malaysia (DOSM) data and a wide range of high-frequency indicators to assess the economy in real time.

items.title: Johor polls: Teo questions rationale for appointed assemblymen, says unity govt delivering step‑by‑step reforms
items.summary: PALOH, July 10 — Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching yesterday questioned the rationale and true motive behind the Johor state government’s decision to create appointed state assemblyman positions. She called on the state government to provide a thorough explanation to avoid negative public perception and to ensure accountability in administration remains intact. Also present were DAP National chairman Gobind Singh Deo and PH candidate for Paloh, Dr Ruban Arumugam.

items.title: High Court adjourns Muhyiddin’s power abuse trial to Monday after death of brother‑in‑law
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The High Court today agreed to postpone former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial, in light of his family member’s death. Before the trial could continue today, Muhyiddin’s lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad applied to the High Court to not proceed with trial today and to resume next Monday, due to “unexpected” news on his client’s brother-in-law’s death. High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin then allowed Muhyiddin’s request to postpone the trial to Monday, also expressing her condolences to Muhyiddin and his family.

items.title: Anwar urges all Malaysians to reject extreme racism and politics of hatred
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all citizens to stop extreme racism and the politics of hatred, and instead prioritise the spirit of unity and mutual respect as Malaysians. Anwar who is also the Pakatan Harapan (PH) Chairman, said that the government under his leadership implements fair policies for all races without neglecting the interests of any community. “I want all citizens to stop extreme racism.

items.title: Penang to gazette first-ever Seberang Perai Local Plan by October, Chow says
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 10 — Penang expects to gazette its first-ever Seberang Perai Local Plan by October this year, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. He said this will mark a major milestone for the state to guide future development on the mainland. He said the public display for the Seberang Perai Local Plan had already concluded and the state was now moving towards gazettement.

items.title: Melaka Form One boy chases classmates with paper cutter after being repeatedly pelted with ice
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, July 10 — A Form One student allegedly chased three classmates with a paper cutter knife after being repeatedly bullied with ice thrown at him during a co curricular session at a secondary school here yesterday. Alor Gajah district police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said initial investigations found the incident occurred at about 2.50pm while a student attendance appreciation programme was taking place in the school’s open hall, Harian Metro (HM) reported. He said two boys, aged 13 and 14, were believed to have repeatedly thrown ice at the suspect, also aged 13.

items.title: Budget 2027 to emphasise sustainable, inclusive, people‑focused growth under Madani Economy, says MOF
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The government will emphasise sustainable, inclusive and people-centric economic growth, in line with the Madani Economy Framework: Empowering the People, in formulating Budget 2027. The Ministry of Finance (MOF) said that under the framework, the government will ensure that every fiscal policy and public expenditure initiative not only supports economic growth but also improves the overall quality of life for the people. “Among the key priorities are strengthening social protection and targeted assistance; continuing the gradual and targeted subsidy reforms; and increasing people’s income and social mobility.
